hypothesis: Incorporating molecular charge as a latent conditioning variable into the MLIP message-passing framework—rather than simply excluding non-neutral structures from training—will enable a single equivariant GNN model to predict energies and forces across multiple charge states with accuracy comparable to charge-specific models, as measured by MAE on a held-out benchmark of charged organic drug-like molecules from SPICE2, while maintaining MD simulation stability equivalent to the neutral-only pre-trained models presented in this work.
